channel.title: eunomia blog
channel.description: Latest writing from eunomia.dev on eBPF, bpftime, AI tracing, and systems research.

item.title: Can an AI Agent Tune the Linux Scheduler? Inside SchedCP
item.description: A parallel Linux kernel build wants throughput. An interactive service wants predictable wake-up latency. A mixed workload may want both, depending on which process is on the critical path. The Linux scheduler sees
item.author: i@littlefisher.me (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: Profiling AI Agents with Semantic Flamegraphs
item.description: End of month, the bill shows the agent spent $3000. What types of work consumed that budget? How much went to code review, how much to debugging, how much to documentation? This question seems simple, but none of the
item.author: 1067852565@qq.com (云微)
i@littlefisher.me (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: ActPlane: Pushing Agent Harness Enforcement Down to Kernel eBPF
item.description: You wrote a rule in CLAUDE.md: "do not run git push." The agent obeyed, and it never called the git tool.
item.author: i@littlefisher.me (LinuxDev9002)
1067852565@qq.com (云微)
linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: Runtime Observability and Enforcement for Opaque AI Agents with eBPF: Beyond Sandboxes and Approvals
item.description: AI coding agents now run for hours, complete entire features end-to-end, optimize production GPU kernels, and merge thousands of pull requests autonomously.
item.author: linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: When CPU Noise Slows Down GPU Inference: Measuring Scheduler and IRQ Impact with eBPF
item.description: GPU inference often looks like a GPU problem, but the CPU still sits on the critical path.
item.author: linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: ACRFence: Preventing Semantic Rollback Attacks in Agent Checkpoint-Restore
item.description: AI agent frameworks are bringing checkpoint/restore, time travel, and rewind into everyday developer workflows.
item.author: linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: AgentCgroup: What Happens When AI Coding Agents Meet OS Resources?
item.description: AI coding agents such as Claude Code, OpenHands, and SWE-agent are increasingly deployed in multi-tenant cloud environments, where they execute diverse tool calls inside sandboxed containers.
item.author: i@littlefisher.me (LinuxDev9002)
linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: Reverse Engineering Claude Code's SSL Traffic with eBPF
item.description: Ever wondered what your AI coding agent is actually sending over the network? As part of our work on AgentSight — an eBPF-powered observability tool that monitors AI agents at system boundaries without modifying their
item.author: linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)

item.title: A Taxonomy of GPU Bugs: 19 Defect Classes for CUDA Verification
item.description: GPU programming introduces a distinct class of correctness and performance challenges that differ fundamentally from traditional CPU-based systems.
item.author: linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)
1067852565@qq.com (云微)

item.title: Architectures for Agent Systems: A Survey of Isolation, Integration, and Governance
item.description: Large Language Model (LLM) based agent systems – software that leverages LLMs to autonomously plan and execute multi-step tasks using external tools – are rapidly moving from proof-of-concept demos into enterprise
item.author: i@littlefisher.me (Littlefisher)

item.title: The GPU Observability Gap: Why We Need eBPF on GPU devices
item.description: As a revolutionary technology that provides programmability in the kernel, eBPF has achieved tremendous success in CPU observability, networking, and security.
item.author: i@littlefisher.me (Littlefisher)
yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)

item.title: NVIDIA Open GPU Kernel Modules Comprehensive Source Code Analysis
item.description: In May 2022, NVIDIA made a decision that would fundamentally alter the landscape of GPU computing on Linux: they open-sourced the kernel-mode components of their GPU driver.
item.author: linuxdev8883@example.com (LinuxDev9002)
i@littlefisher.me (Littlefisher)
yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)

item.title: Understanding iaprof: A Deep Dive into AI/GPU Flame Graph Profiling
item.description: An exploration of Intel's innovative profiling tool that bridges the gap between CPU and GPU execution
item.author: yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)

item.title: AgentSight: Keeping Your AI Agents Under Control with eBPF-Powered System Observability
item.description: Picture this: your AI agent is autonomously writing code and executing commands, but you have no idea what it's actually doing.
item.author: yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)

item.title: Profiling and Tracing Tools Across System Layers and Architectures
item.description: Profiling and tracing are complementary techniques for analyzing software performance and behavior.
item.author: yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)

item.title: The Modern Memory Testing Arsenal -- A Complete Guide to Benchmarking Tools for Next-Gen Memory Systems
item.description: Memory systems are evolving rapidly. From traditional DDR DRAM to high-bandwidth memory (HBM), persistent memory (PMEM), and the emerging Compute Express Link (CXL) technology, today's systems feature complex
item.author: yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)

item.title: Observability, Profiling, and Debugging in Systems Conference (2015–2025)
item.description: This survey reviews over a decade (2015–2025) of research on observability, profiling, and debugging techniques in computer systems, focusing on main-track papers from OSDI, SOSP, and EuroSys.
item.author: yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)

item.title: Checkpoint/Restore Systems: Evolution, Techniques, and Applications in AI Agents
item.description: Checkpoint/restore (C/R) technology – the ability to save a running program’s state to persistent storage and later resume execution from that point – has long been a cornerstone of fault tolerance and process
item.author: yt.xyxx@gmail.com (officeyutong)

item.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights
item.description: The Association for Computing Machinery's Architectural Support for Programming Languages and Operating Systems (ASPLOS) conference is a premier venue where researchers present cutting-edge work spanning computer
item.author: yt.xyxx@gmail.com (officeyutong)

item.title: EuroSys 2025 Paper Summaries and Analysis
item.description: This post offers a detailed examination of papers accepted at EuroSys 2025, one of the premier conferences in computer systems research.
item.author: yt.xyxx@gmail.com (officeyutong)

item.title: GPU Profiling Under the Hood: An Implementation-Focused Survey of Modern Accelerator Tracing Tools
item.description: Profiling and tracing heterogeneous accelerators (GPUs, DPUs, and APUs) is crucial for optimizing performance in modern systems.
item.author: yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)
yt.xyxx@gmail.com (officeyutong)

item.title: The Accelerator Toolkit: A Review of Profiling and Tracing for GPUs and other co-processor
item.description: Modern computing increasingly relies on specialized accelerators – notably GPUs, DPUs, and APUs – to handle diverse workloads.
item.author: yt.xyxx@gmail.com (officeyutong)

item.title: OS-Level Challenges in LLM Inference and Optimizations
item.description: Large Language Model (LLM) inference pushes computing systems to their limits, not only in raw compute but also in how the operating system (OS) manages resources.
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)
1067852565@qq.com (云微)

item.title: WASI and the WebAssembly Component Model: Current Status
item.description: WebAssembly (WASM) has evolved from a browser-based technology into a promising runtime for server and embedded applications.
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)
1067852565@qq.com (云微)

item.title: eBPF Ecosystem Progress in 2024–2025: A Technical Deep Dive
item.description: Extended Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF) continues to rapidly evolve, cementing its role as a cornerstone for operating system extensibility.
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)

item.title: Security Vulnerabilities Study in Software Extensions and Plugins
item.description: Software extensions and plugins allow customization and added features across many systems – from web servers and databases to browsers, IDEs, and CMS platforms.
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)

item.title: Can LLMs understand Linux kernel? A New AI-Powered Approach to Understanding Large Codebases
item.description: Ever tried diving into a massive codebase like the Linux kernel and felt like you were swimming in an ocean of code with no land in sight?
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)
1067852565@qq.com (云微)

item.title: Building High-Performance Userspace eBPF VMs with LLVM
item.description: We are excited to introduce llvmbpf, a new project aimed at empowering developers with a high-performance, multi-architecture eBPF virtual machine (VM) that leverages the LLVM framework for Just-In-Time (JIT) and
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)
1067852565@qq.com (云微)

item.title: The Past, Present, and Future of eBPF and Its Path to Revolutionizing Systems
item.description: This blog post mainly references Alexei Starovoitov's presentation "Modernize BPF for the Next 10 Years" at BPFConf 2024.
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)
1067852565@qq.com (云微)
yunwei356@gmail.com (Yusheng Zheng)

item.title: Simplifying Kernel Programming: The LLM-Powered eBPF Tool
item.description: Kernel programming can be intimidating, requiring deep knowledge of operating system internals and programming constraints.
item.author: yusen356@gmail.com (weekendfish)
yunwei356@gmail.com (yunwei37)
